data_sources.carbon_intensity: Cloud Carbon Footprint project (EPA eGRID2023, EEA, IEA), cross-referenced with CarbonFootprint.com 2024 factors and Electricity Maps methodology
data_sources.instance_energy: SPECpower database, Intel/AMD/Ampere published TDP values, cloud provider documentation
data_sources.pue: AWS 2024 Sustainability Report (global PUE 1.15), Microsoft 2023 sustainability data, Google 2024 environmental report
data_sources.epa_egrid: eGRID2023 (released January 2025, revised June 2025) for US subregion emission factors
data_sources.ccf_project: Cloud Carbon Footprint v0.x (https://www.cloudcarbonfootprint.org) for international region factors
data_sources.carbonfootprint_com: CarbonFootprint.com international electricity factors 2024 for UK, Germany, France

methodology_notes: Carbon intensity values represent annual average grid carbon intensity for each region's geographic location, NOT accounting for cloud provider renewable energy purchases (which use market-based accounting via RECs/PPAs). Actual intensity varies by time of day and season. For US regions, sub-grid factors (e.g., CAMX for California, BPA for Oregon) are used instead of broader NERC region averages (e.g., WECC) for greater accuracy. PUE (Power Usage Effectiveness) is applied as a multiplier to account for datacenter cooling and overhead. AWS reports global PUE of 1.15 (2024), with regional variation: Europe 1.11, North America 1.14, Asia Pacific 1.27.

verification_notes.us_regions: US values use EPA eGRID2023 subregion factors. us-west-1 uses CAMX (California ISO), us-west-2 uses BPA (Bonneville Power) rather than WECC-wide average, which is more accurate for these specific locations.
verification_notes.canada: ca-central-1 uses Quebec provincial grid factor (hydro-dominated ~22 gCO2/kWh), not Canada national average (~115 gCO2/kWh), since the datacenter is in Montreal.
verification_notes.international: International values sourced from Cloud Carbon Footprint project (IEA-based) cross-referenced with CarbonFootprint.com 2024 factors where available.
verification_notes.renewable_percentages: Approximate annual averages from national/regional grid operator data. Do not reflect time-of-day variation.
verification_notes.aws_renewable_claims: AWS claims 100% renewable energy matching (2024) via market-based accounting. This does NOT change grid carbon intensity values, which represent the physical grid mix.

us-east-1.name: N. Virginia
us-east-1.source: EPA eGRID2023 SRVC subregion, confirmed by CCF (0.000379069 mtCO2/kWh)
us-east-1.notes: PJM Interconnection (Dominion Virginia), coal/gas/nuclear mix. One of the highest-carbon US AWS regions.

us-east-2.name: Ohio
us-east-2.source: EPA eGRID2023 RFCW subregion, confirmed by CCF (0.000410608 mtCO2/kWh)
us-east-2.notes: PJM Interconnection (AEP Ohio), significant coal and gas generation.

us-west-1.name: N. California
us-west-1.source: EPA eGRID2023 CAMX subregion (428.5 lbs/MWh = 194 gCO2/kWh direct combustion; ~210 lifecycle). Sub-grid of WECC.
us-west-1.notes: CAISO grid, growing solar and wind portfolio. More accurate than WECC-wide average of 322.

us-west-2.name: Oregon
us-west-2.source: EPA eGRID2023 BPA/NWPP subregion. Bonneville Power Administration hydro-dominated. Sub-grid of WECC.
us-west-2.notes: Predominantly Columbia River hydroelectric. One of the lowest-carbon AWS regions globally. WECC-wide average (322) is misleading.

ca-central-1.name: Canada (Montreal)
ca-central-1.source: Hydro-Quebec grid data. CCF uses Canada-wide 115.4 gCO2/kWh which is inaccurate for Quebec specifically.
ca-central-1.notes: Quebec hydro-dominated grid. Extremely low carbon intensity. National average (~115) is misleading due to Alberta/Saskatchewan coal/gas.

eu-west-1.name: Ireland
eu-west-1.source: CCF (0.000305 mtCO2/kWh), IEA Ireland data. EirGrid reports 300-320 range for recent years.
eu-west-1.notes: EirGrid, increasing wind capacity (target 80% by 2030) but still significant natural gas generation.

eu-west-2.name: London
eu-west-2.source: National Grid ESO 2024 annual average (125 gCO2/kWh measured). Using 150 as conservative estimate. Wind now 30% of UK generation, coal below 1%.
eu-west-2.notes: UK grid decarbonized rapidly. National Grid ESO measured 125 gCO2/kWh in 2024. Updated April 2026.

eu-west-3.name: Paris
eu-west-3.source: RTE France 2024 annual review (21.7 gCO2eq/kWh direct, ~30 lifecycle). Nuclear 67%, hydro 14%, CO2-free 95% of mix.
eu-west-3.notes: 2024 was a record year for France -- nuclear recovery to 361.7 TWh, hydro highest since 2013. Updated April 2026.

eu-central-1.name: Frankfurt
eu-central-1.source: Ember Global Electricity Review 2025 (~298 gCO2/kWh for Germany 2024), CarbonFootprint.com 2025 (380). Conservative estimate of 310.
eu-central-1.notes: German grid continuing to decarbonize -- coal declining, renewables growing. Updated April 2026.

eu-north-1.name: Stockholm
eu-north-1.source: CCF (0.0000088 mtCO2/kWh = 8.8 gCO2/kWh). Swedish grid is hydro + nuclear dominated.
eu-north-1.notes: Swedish grid, hydro and nuclear dominated. Among the cleanest grids globally. Lowest carbon AWS region in Europe.

ap-southeast-1.name: Singapore
ap-southeast-1.source: CCF (0.00049453 mtCO2/kWh). Singapore EMA data suggests 410-430 for direct combustion; lifecycle ~495.
ap-southeast-1.notes: Almost entirely natural gas generation (~95%). Limited renewable potential due to small land area. Using lifecycle value for consistency.

ap-southeast-2.name: Sydney
ap-southeast-2.source: CCF (0.00058023 mtCO2/kWh). NSW grid still coal-heavy despite growing renewables.
ap-southeast-2.notes: NSW grid, significant black coal generation. One of the higher-carbon AWS regions. Transitioning with large-scale solar/wind.

ap-northeast-1.name: Tokyo
ap-northeast-1.source: CCF (0.00043984 mtCO2/kWh). TEPCO grid, post-Fukushima nuclear restarts gradually lowering intensity.
ap-northeast-1.notes: TEPCO grid, LNG gas and coal dominant with gradual nuclear restart. Declining from ~500+ post-Fukushima levels.

ap-northeast-2.name: Seoul
ap-northeast-2.source: CCF (0.00047739 mtCO2/kWh). Korean grid is coal+nuclear+LNG.
ap-northeast-2.notes: Korean grid, coal and nuclear mix with growing LNG. Low renewable penetration.

ap-south-1.name: Mumbai
ap-south-1.source: CCF uses 951.8 gCO2/kWh which appears high; IEA India average ~700-710 gCO2/kWh (2023). Using IEA-aligned value.
ap-south-1.notes: Indian grid, coal-heavy (~70% of generation). Growing solar capacity but still among highest-carbon regions globally.

sa-east-1.name: São Paulo
sa-east-1.source: CCF (0.00006398 mtCO2/kWh). Brazilian grid is predominantly hydroelectric.
sa-east-1.notes: Brazilian grid, ~65% hydro + wind + biomass. Very low carbon. One of the cleanest AWS regions.

me-south-1.name: Bahrain
me-south-1.source: CCF (0.00050549 mtCO2/kWh). Gulf states are almost entirely fossil fuel powered.
me-south-1.notes: Almost entirely natural gas generation. Minimal renewable deployment.

af-south-1.name: Cape Town
af-south-1.source: CCF (0.00075744 mtCO2/kWh). Eskom grid is heavily coal-dependent.
af-south-1.notes: South African Eskom grid, heavily coal-dependent (~80%+). Highest carbon intensity among AWS regions.

eastus.name: East US (Virginia)
eastus.notes: Same Virginia/SRVC grid as AWS us-east-1.

centralus.name: Central US (Iowa)
centralus.source: ESTIMATED from MISO grid data. Iowa has significant wind capacity.
centralus.notes: MISO grid, significant wind capacity in Iowa.

westus2.name: West US 2 (Washington)
westus2.notes: Pacific Northwest hydro grid (BPA). Very low carbon.

northeurope.name: North Europe (Ireland)
northeurope.notes: Same Irish grid as AWS eu-west-1.

westeurope.name: West Europe (Netherlands)
westeurope.source: ESTIMATED from Dutch grid operator TenneT data.
westeurope.notes: Dutch grid, gas-heavy with growing offshore wind.

uksouth.name: UK South (London)
uksouth.notes: UK National Grid. National Grid ESO 2024 measured 125 gCO2/kWh. Conservative 150. Updated April 2026.

germanywestcentral.name: Germany West Central (Frankfurt)
germanywestcentral.notes: German grid. Ember 2024 ~298, conservative 310. Updated April 2026.

francecentral.name: France Central (Paris)
francecentral.notes: French grid. RTE 2024: 21.7 direct, ~30 lifecycle. Updated April 2026.

swedencentral.name: Sweden Central (Gavle)
swedencentral.notes: Swedish hydro/nuclear grid. CCF value. Among the cleanest globally.

canadacentral.name: Canada Central (Toronto)
canadacentral.source: Ontario grid data. Nuclear (~60%) and hydro (~25%) dominated.
canadacentral.notes: Ontario grid, nuclear and hydro dominated. Low carbon.

southeastasia.name: Southeast Asia (Singapore)
southeastasia.notes: Singapore gas grid. CCF lifecycle value.

us-central1.name: Iowa
us-central1.notes: MISO Iowa, significant wind. Google reports 97%+ CFE matching here.

us-east1.name: South Carolina
us-east1.source: ESTIMATED from Duke Energy Carolinas generation mix.

us-west1.name: Oregon
us-west1.notes: Pacific NW hydro (BPA). Google reports 90%+ CFE matching here.

us-west4.name: Las Vegas
us-west4.source: ESTIMATED from Nevada Power/NWPP grid data.

northamerica-northeast1.name: Montreal
northamerica-northeast1.notes: Quebec hydro grid. Among the cleanest globally.

europe-west1.name: Belgium
europe-west1.source: ESTIMATED from Belgian grid operator Elia data.

europe-west2.name: London
europe-west2.notes: UK National Grid. ESO 2024 measured 125, conservative 150. Updated April 2026.

europe-west3.name: Frankfurt
europe-west3.notes: German grid. Ember 2024 ~298, conservative 310. Updated April 2026.

europe-west4.name: Netherlands
europe-west4.notes: Dutch grid, gas-heavy with growing offshore wind.

europe-north1.name: Finland
europe-north1.source: ESTIMATED from Fingrid data. Nuclear + hydro + wind dominated.
europe-north1.notes: Finnish grid, nuclear/hydro/wind. Google reports low carbon CFE matching.

asia-southeast1.name: Singapore
asia-southeast1.notes: Singapore gas grid. CCF lifecycle value.

m7g.notes: Latest ARM-based, best energy efficiency in M-family.

c7g.notes: Latest ARM compute optimized. Best perf-per-watt.

t3a.notes: AMD burstable. Slightly lower power than t3.

t4g.notes: ARM burstable. Lowest power in T-family.

Dsv5.notes: General purpose v5 with premium storage.

Fv2.notes: Compute optimized. No ARM equivalent yet.

Dpsv5.notes: ARM-based general purpose. Best energy efficiency.

n2d-standard.notes: General purpose AMD. Slightly more efficient than Intel.

t2a-standard.notes: ARM-based. Best energy efficiency on GCP.

m2-megamem.notes: Ultra high memory. Very high power draw.
